name: GSI TECHNOLOGY INC
summary: GSI Technology Inc. develops in-place associative computing solutions for AI and high-performance computing markets, including its Gemini APU (Associative Processing Unit) chip family supporting similarity search and Boolean processing. The company also manufactures high-speed synchronous SRAM products for networking, telecommunications, test and measurement, military/defense, aerospace, and space applications. GSI operates a fabless business model and serves OEM customers including KYEC and Nokia.

products.name: Gemini-I APU
products.description: First-generation associative processing unit in full production, supporting similarity search and compute-in-memory AI applications.

products.name: Gemini-II APU
products.description: Second-generation compute-in-memory chip with order-of-magnitude improved performance over Gemini-I, targeting general market in mid-2025.

products.name: APU2 (Next-Generation Associative Processing Unit-2)
products.description: Advanced non-Von-Neumann compute-in-memory IC developed for U.S. Space Force and Air Force applications.

products.name: High-Speed Synchronous SRAM
products.description: High-density synchronous SRAM products for networking, telecommunications, test and measurement equipment.

products.name: Radiation-Hardened SRAM
products.description: Space-grade radiation-tolerant and radiation-hardened SRAM for military/defense and aerospace markets.

products.name: APU Software Stack / Compiler
products.description: Integrated framework with APIs, libraries, and Python-supported compiler for customer programming of Gemini APU chips.

products.name: APU as-a-Service
products.description: Cloud-based APU access via Amazon Web Services, Azure, or Google Cloud Storage for similarity search and AI workloads.

partnerships.description: SBIR Direct-to-Phase II award for APU2 development for space-based big data processing; milestone payments up to ~$1.25M.

partnerships.partner_name: AFWERX / U.S. Air Force Research Laboratory
partnerships.description: SBIR Direct-to-Phase II contract of $1.1M to develop compute-in-memory algorithms for Air Force AI applications.
Potential DoD SBIR contract up to $250,000 to develop edge computing AI solutions using Gemini-II technology.
Long-term OEM customer for high-speed synchronous SRAM products.
Long-term OEM customer for high-speed synchronous SRAM products.
Hired as strategic and financial advisor for GSI's board-led strategic alternatives review process initiated May 2024.
